Articles & Press.Title: Alphabets' Colin Ward: "It began when I was born. Or before. Or scratch that it just began five minutes ago."
Articles & Press.Summary: Interviewing Alphabets (Colin Ward) is similar to listening to his music: you're put through the ringer of an obnoxious charm, simultaneously witty and grating, aggressive and inviting. Combining both the underground music scene with demanding academic ambitions, the hyper and endlessly talented Colin Ward keeps himself busy with a creative...

Articles & Press.Title: Another 100 Colorado Creatives: Colin Ward
Articles & Press.Summary: #38: Colin Ward Colin Ward lives to create, juggling disciplines with grace at Rhinoceropolis, one of Denver's most successful DIY arts spaces (it celebrates its tenth anniversary this year). No prima donna, the musician, producer, composer, performer, percussionist, painter, video artist, curator and events designer seeks to bring talented people...

Articles & Press.Title: Colin Ward's Magic Will Be Missed
Articles & Press.Summary: Colin Ward's friends remember him as "magic." There will be a Service of Remembrance on February 10, 2018.

Articles & Press.Title: Pushing Through the “Growth” of Denver: article and movie
Articles & Press.Summary: I’ve always wanted to tell stories about where I’m from largely because of how underrepresented Denver is in many arenas. Denver’s music scene is a great example of this: people f…

Articles & Press.Title: Pictureplane's Travis Egedy Remembers Rhinoceropolis and Colin Ward in a New Music Video
Articles & Press.Summary: Travis Egedy remembers Rhinoceropolis and the late artist Colin Ward in a new music video for the song “Color Spectrum (Tokyo Drift)” off Pictureplane's new album, Degenerate.

Articles & Press.Title: Rhinoceropolis and Glob Struggle to Reopen After Denver Shut Them Down
Articles & Press.Summary: John Golter had no reason to think that he and his friends would be homeless by nightfall.

Articles & Press.Title: A New Compilation Celebrates the Futuristic Rave Music of the Late Colin Ward
Articles & Press.Author: Sam Goldner
Articles & Press.Summary: A new collection of the late artist's music was assembled by his friends and collaborators.

Articles & Press.Title: Colin Ward Memorial Tribute at Meow Wolf on February 22

Biographical Summary.Value: alphabets; killd by (originally 'Killd By Tha Groove'); Pocket Dove
Hamden, Connecticut; moved to Denver, Colorado in 1999
Denver DIY community; resident of Rhinoceropolis (Denver DIY art/music venue)
Drums, keyboards, electronic production, marimba, piano, guitar, flute, clarinet (studied from age 9)
killd by, alphabets, and street drumming on Denver's 16th Street Mall; Aquakota installation at Meow Wolf Convergence Station
2008–2013 (47 releases on Bandcamp, independent labels, cassettes, CDs)
2015–2017 (10 releases on Bandcamp, independent labels, CDs)
Sept 2015 – Dec 2016 (470 iPhone recordings in CRFW Archive)
CRFW Fund — administered by CAST (cast-sf.org); provides emergency grants to Denver DIY artists

Associated Projects.Notes: Successor to alphabets; 2015–2017; originally 'Killd By Tha Groove'
Early music project — see BIEBER JERBOA_2010_[phonebooks] in archive

Associated Projects.Collaborators: Luke Leavitt, Luke Thinnes, Ben Donehower
